items.summary: With the management plan complete, the focus at Bears Ears is on day-to-day care, coordination, and stewardship guided by Tribal leadership and carried out in collaboration with federal agencies.

The subject lands are found on the west side of […]

items.title: Re-Establishment of the Hunting and Shooting Sports Conservation and Access Council; Request for Nominations
items.summary: The U.S. Department of the Interior (DOI) is re-establishing and seeking member nominations to the Hunting and Shooting Sports Conservation and Access Council (Council). The Council replaces and changes the name of the previous Hunting and Wildlife Conservation Council. The Secretary of the Interior (Secretary), after consultation with the General Services Administration, is re-establishing the Council charter for 2 years. items.title: Two Beneficiaries to Benefit from November Online Auction
items.summary: SITLA offers eight properties at its online land sale auction November 12-18, 2020. Trust land parcels range from one to 651 acres and are located in Daggett, Garfield, Iron, Uintah, and Utah counties. Proceeds from the sale will be deposited into the permanent endowments for public education and Utah School for the Deaf. Visit the […]

items.title: SITLA Trust Land Auction Earns $344K for Schools, UofU College of Mines
items.summary: SITLA held its biannual public auction this week generating $343,900 for two beneficiary endowments, public schools and the University of Utah College of Mines and Earth Sciences. The seven parcels sold totaled a combined 315 acres and are located in Duchesne, Emery, Garfield, and Sevier counties. By law, SITLA retains the mineral estate. All proceeds […]

items.title: SITLA Board Meets to Discuss Trust Lands Within New Monument
items.summary: The Utah School and Institutional Trust Lands Administration (SITLA) Board of Trustees met Friday, January 6 to discuss strategy, concerns, and next steps following the designation of the Bears Ears National Monument. Prior to moving into a 2.5-hour closed session, Trustees heard from Merri Black Shumway, a San Juan County School District board member and […]

items.title: Development Partner Sought for Resort Project Near Bullfrog Marina
items.summary: Now through January 26, 2017, the Utah School and Institutional Trust Lands Administration (SITLA) is accepting statements of interest and qualifications from prospective partners for a resort development at the Bullfrog Marina entrance to Glen Canyon National Recreation Area in Garfield County, Utah. Through this Request For Qualifications (RFQ), SITLA seeks a partner to develop […]

items.title: SITLA Land Auction Nets $3.6 Million for Beneficiaries
items.summary: SITLA sold 11 parcels of trust lands at its biannual public auction May 25, 2016, earning $3,657,050 for public schools, Utah State University, Miners Hospital at the University of Utah Medical Center, the Utah State Hospital, and state reservoirs. The lands sold include recreation, agricultural, grazing, and development parcels in Box Elder, Daggett, Emery, Grand, Garfield, San Juan, […]

items.title: SITLA Land Auction Earns $673K For Public Schools
items.summary: The School and Institutional Trust Lands Administration (SITLA) sold six trust land parcels at its public auction October 28, earning $673,500 for Utah’s Permanent School Fund. Two parcels in San Juan County sold for a total of $345,000; a 640-acre parcel in Millard County sold for $300,000; and three one-acre parcels in Garfield County sold […]

items.title: School Trust Lands in Garfield, Millard, and San Juan Counties Available
items.summary: SITLA will sell seven parcels of school trust land at its biannual public auction at 11 a.m. Wednesday, October 28 in the SITLA Board Room at 675 East 500 South, Suite 600 in Salt Lake City. Widstoe Townsite parcels A, B, and C are approximately one acre each and located in John’s Valley on State […]

items.title: SITLA Land Auction Earns $1.9M for Public Schools
items.summary: The School and Institutional Trust Lands Administration (SITLA) sold six trust land parcels at its biannual public auction June 3, earning $1,957,000 for Utah’s Permanent School Fund. Four separate parcels in Garfield County sold for a total of $647,000; a parcel in Millard County sold for $260,000; and a large 1,500-acre parcel in Grand County […]
